metadata.name: stg_stripe__account
metadata.comment: Properties belonging to a Stripe account.

business_profile_mcc.name: business_profile_mcc
business_profile_mcc.comment: The merchant category code for the account. MCCs are used to classify businesses based on the goods or services they provide.

charges_enabled.name: charges_enabled
charges_enabled.comment: Whether the account can create live charges.

company_address_city.name: company_address_city
company_address_city.comment: City, district, suburb, town, or village.

company_address_line_1.name: company_address_line_1
company_address_line_1.comment: Address line 1 (e.g., street, PO Box, or company name).

company_address_line_2.name: company_address_line_2
company_address_line_2.comment: Address line 2 (e.g., apartment, suite, unit, or building).

company_phone.name: company_phone
company_phone.comment: The company’s phone number (used for verification).

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.comment: Time at which the account was connected. Measured in seconds since the Unix epoch.

default_currency.name: default_currency
default_currency.comment: Three-letter ISO currency code representing the default currency for the account. This must be a currency that Stripe supports in the account’s country.

email.name: email
email.comment: An email address associated with the account. You can treat this as metadata; it is not used for authentication or messaging account holders.

is_deleted.name: is_deleted
is_deleted.comment: Boolean of whether account has been deleted. Accounts created using test-mode keys can be deleted at any time. Standard accounts created using live-mode keys cannot be deleted. Custom or Express accounts created using live-mode keys can only be deleted once all balances are zero.

metadata.name: metadata
metadata.comment: Set of key-value pairs that you can attach to an object. This can be useful for storing additional information about the object in a structured format. Individual keys can be unset by posting an empty value to them. All keys can be unset by posting an empty value to metadata.

is_payouts_enabled.name: is_payouts_enabled
is_payouts_enabled.comment: Boolean of whether payouts are enabled.

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The source where this data was pulled from. If you are making use of the `union_schemas` variable, this will be the source schema. If you are making use of the `union_databases` variable, this will be the source database. If you are not unioning together multiple sources, this will be an empty string.

metadata.name: stg_stripe__balance_transaction
metadata.comment: Balance transactions represent funds moving through your Stripe account. They're created for every type of transaction that comes into or flows out of your Stripe account balance.

amount.name: amount
amount.comment: Gross amount of the transaction. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

fee.name: fee
fee.comment: fees (in currency units) paid for this transaction. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

net.name: net
net.comment: Net amount of the transaction. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

available_on.name: available_on
available_on.comment: The date the transaction’s net funds will become available in the Stripe balance.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.comment: Timestamp of when the transaction was created, in UTC.

connected_account_id.name: connected_account_id
connected_account_id.comment: The ID of the account connected to the transaction.

currency.name: currency
currency.comment: Three-letter ISO currency code, in lowercase.

description.name: description
description.comment: An arbitrary string attached to the object. Often useful for displaying to users.

exchange_rate.name: exchange_rate
exchange_rate.comment: The exchange rate used, if applicable, for this transaction. Specifically, if money was converted from currency A to currency B, then the amount in currency A, times exchange_rate, would be the amount in currency B.

reporting_category.name: reporting_category
reporting_category.comment: Improves on the type field by providing a more-useful grouping for most finance and reporting purposes.

source.name: source
source.comment: The Stripe object to which this transaction is related.

status.name: status
status.comment: If the transaction’s net funds are available in the Stripe balance yet. Either 'available' or 'pending'.

type.name: type
type.comment: The type of transaction.  Possible values are adjustment, advance, advance_funding, application_fee, application_fee_refund, charge, connect_collection_transfer, issuing_authorization_hold, issuing_authorization_release, issuing_dispute, issuing_transaction, payment, payment_failure_refund, payment_refund, payout, payout_cancel, payout_failure, refund, refund_failure, reserve_transaction, reserved_funds, stripe_fee, stripe_fx_fee, tax_fee, topup, topup_reversal, transfer, transfer_cancel, transfer_failure, or transfer_refund.

source_relation.comment: The source where this data was pulled from. If you are making use of the `union_schemas` variable, this will be the source schema. If you are making use of the `union_databases` variable, this will be the source database. If you are not unioning together multiple sources, this will be an empty string.

metadata.name: stg_stripe__card
metadata.comment: Details of a credit card that has been saved to the system.

account_id.name: account_id
account_id.comment: ID of account that this card is associated with.

card_address_city.name: card_address_city
card_address_city.comment: City, district, suburb, town, or village.

card_address_line_1.name: card_address_line_1
card_address_line_1.comment: Address line 1 (e.g., street, PO Box, or company name).

card_address_line_2.name: card_address_line_2
card_address_line_2.comment: Address line 2 (e.g., apartment, suite, unit, or building).

wallet_type.name: wallet_type
wallet_type.comment: The type of the card wallet, one of amex_express_checkout, apple_pay, google_pay, masterpass, samsung_pay, or visa_checkout. An additional hash is included on the Wallet subhash with a name matching this value. It contains additional information specific to the card wallet type.

brand.name: brand
brand.comment: Card brand. Can be American Express, Diners Club, Discover, JCB, MasterCard, UnionPay, Visa, or Unknown.

country.name: country
country.comment: Two-letter ISO code representing the country of the card. You could use this attribute to get a sense of the international breakdown of cards you’ve collected.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.comment: Timestamp of when the entry for the card was created

customer_id.name: customer_id
customer_id.comment: The customer that this card belongs to.  NULL if belongs to an account or recipient.

recipient.name: recipient
recipient.comment: The recipient that this card belongs to. NULL if the card belongs to a customer or account instead.

funding.name: funding
funding.comment: Card funding type. Can be credit, debit, prepaid, or unknown.

metadata.name: stg_stripe__charge
metadata.comment: To charge a credit or a debit card, you create a Charge object. You can retrieve and refund individual charges as well as list all charges. Charges are identified by a unique, random ID.

amount.name: amount
amount.comment: Amount intended to be collected by this payment. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

amount_refunded.name: amount_refunded
amount_refunded.comment: The amount of the charge, if any, that has been refunded. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

application_fee_amount.name: application_fee_amount
application_fee_amount.comment: The amount of the application fee (if any) for the charge. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

balance_transaction_id.name: balance_transaction_id
balance_transaction_id.comment: ID of the balance transaction that describes the impact of this charge on your account balance (not including refunds or disputes).

is_captured.name: is_captured
is_captured.comment: If the charge was created without capturing, this Boolean represents whether it is still uncaptured or has since been captured.

card_id.name: card_id
card_id.comment: ID of the card that was charged.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.comment: Timestamp of when the charge took place.

connected_account_id.name: connected_account_id
connected_account_id.comment: ID of account connected for this charge.

customer_id.name: customer_id
customer_id.comment: ID of the customer this charge is for if one exists.

description.name: description
description.comment: An arbitrary string attached to the object. Often useful for displaying to users.

failure_code.name: failure_code
failure_code.comment: Error code explaining reason for charge failure if available.

failure_message.name: failure_message
failure_message.comment: Message to user further explaining reason for charge failure if available.

metadata.name: metadata
metadata.comment: Custom metadata added to the record, in JSON string format

is_paid.name: is_paid
is_paid.comment: true if the charge succeeded, or was successfully authorized for later capture.

payment_intent_id.name: payment_intent_id
payment_intent_id.comment: ID of the PaymentIntent associated with this charge, if one exists.

payment_method_id.name: payment_method_id
payment_method_id.comment: Unique identifier for the payment method object used in this charge.

receipt_email.name: receipt_email
receipt_email.comment: This is the email address that the receipt for this charge was sent to.

receipt_number.name: receipt_number
receipt_number.comment: This is the transaction number that appears on email receipts sent for this charge.

is_refunded.name: is_refunded
is_refunded.comment: Whether the charge has been fully refunded. If the charge is only partially refunded, this attribute will still be false.

status.name: status
status.comment: The status of the payment is either succeeded, pending, or failed.

shipping_address_city.name: shipping_address_city
shipping_address_city.comment: City, district, suburb, town, or village.

shipping_address_line_1.name: shipping_address_line_1
shipping_address_line_1.comment: Address line 1 (e.g., street, PO Box, or company name).

shipping_address_line_2.name: shipping_address_line_2
shipping_address_line_2.comment: Address line 2 (e.g., apartment, suite, unit, or building).

shipping_carrier.name: shipping_carrier
shipping_carrier.comment: The delivery service that shipped a physical product, such as Fedex, UPS, USPS, etc.

shipping_tracking_number.name: shipping_tracking_number
shipping_tracking_number.comment: The tracking number for a physical product, obtained from the delivery service. If multiple tracking numbers were

source_id.comment: ID of the source associated. Source objects allow you to accept a variety of payment methods. They represent a cu

source_transfer.name: source_transfer
source_transfer.comment: The transfer ID which created this charge. Only present if the charge came from another Stripe account.

statement_descriptor.name: statement_descriptor
statement_descriptor.comment: Extra information about a source. This will appear on your customer’s statement every time you charge the source.

invoice_id.name: invoice_id
invoice_id.comment: The id of the invoice associated with this charge.

calculated_statement_descriptor.name: calculated_statement_descriptor
calculated_statement_descriptor.comment: The full statement descriptor that is passed to card networks, and that is displayed on your customers’ credit card and bank statements. Allows you to see what the statement descriptor looks like after the static and dynamic portions are combined.

redeem_by.comment: The date by which the coupon must be redeemed.

duration.comment: The duration of the coupon. Typically "forever," "once," or "repeating."

duration_in_months.name: duration_in_months

amount_off.comment: The amount taken off the total price in the selected currency.

max_redemptions.comment: The maximum number of times this coupon can be redeemed.

metadata.name: stg_stripe__credit_note
metadata.comment: Credit notes are documents that decrease the amount owed on an invoice. They’re the only way to adjust the amount of a finalized invoice other than voiding and recreating the invoice.

credit_note_amount.name: credit_note_amount
credit_note_amount.comment: The integer amount in currency units representing the total amount of the credit note, including tax.  "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

credit_note_discount_amount.name: credit_note_discount_amount
credit_note_discount_amount.comment: The integer amount in currency units representing the total amount of discount that was credited. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

credit_note_subtotal.name: credit_note_subtotal
credit_note_subtotal.comment: The integer amount in currency units representing the amount of the credit note, excluding tax and invoice level discounts. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

credit_note_total.name: credit_note_total
credit_note_total.comment: The integer amount in currency units representing the total amount of the credit note, including tax and all discount. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.comment: Timestamp of the when the credit_note was created.

credit_note_currency.name: credit_note_currency
credit_note_currency.comment: The currency of the charge. Three-letter ISO currency code, in lowercase.

memo.name: memo
memo.comment: Customer-facing text that appears on the credit note PDF.

metadata.name: metadata
metadata.comment: Set of key-value pairs that you can attach to an object. This can be useful for storing additional information about the object in a structured format.

credit_note_number.name: credit_note_number
credit_note_number.comment: A unique number that identifies this particular credit note and appears on the PDF of the credit note and its associated invoice.

pdf.name: pdf
pdf.comment: The link to download the PDF of the credit note.

credit_note_reason.name: credit_note_reason
credit_note_reason.comment: Reason for issuing this credit note, one of duplicate, fraudulent, order_change, or product_unsatisfactory

credit_note_status.name: credit_note_status
credit_note_status.comment: Status of this credit note, one of issued or void.

credit_note_type.name: credit_note_type
credit_note_type.comment: Type of this credit note, one of pre_payment or post_payment. A pre_payment credit note means it was issued when the invoice was open. A post_payment credit note means it was issued when the invoice was paid.

voided_at.name: voided_at
voided_at.comment: The time that the credit note was voided.

customer_balance_transaction.name: customer_balance_transaction
customer_balance_transaction.comment: Customer balance transaction related to this credit note.

invoice_id.name: invoice_id
invoice_id.comment: The id of the invoice associated with this credit note.

refund_id.name: refund_id
refund_id.comment: The id of the refund associated with this credit note.

source_relation.comment: The source where this data was pulled from. If you are making use of the `union_schemas` variable, this will be the source schema. If you are making use of the `union_databases` variable, this will be the source database. If you are not unioning together multiple sources, this will be an empty string.

credit_note_id.comment: The ID of the credit note this item is a part of.

credit_note_line_item_amount.name: credit_note_line_item_amount
credit_note_line_item_amount.comment: The integer amount in currency units representing the gross amount being credited for this line item, excluding (exclusive) tax and discounts. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. credit_note_line_item_description.name: credit_note_line_item_description
credit_note_line_item_description.comment: Description of the item being credited.

quantity.name: quantity
quantity.comment: The number of units of product being credited.

credit_note_line_item_type.name: credit_note_line_item_type
credit_note_line_item_type.comment: The type of the credit note line item, one of invoice_line_item or custom_line_item. When the type is invoice_line_item there is an additional invoice_line_item property on the resource the value of which is the id of the credited line item on the invoice.

credit_note_line_item_unit_amount.name: credit_note_line_item_unit_amount
credit_note_line_item_unit_amount.comment: The cost of each unit of product being credited. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

metadata.name: stg_stripe__customer
metadata.comment: Customer objects allow you to perform recurring charges, and to track multiple charges, that are associated with the same customer.

account_balance.name: account_balance
account_balance.comment: Current balance, if any, being stored on the customer. If negative, the customer has credit to apply to their next invoice. If positive, the customer has an amount owed that will be added to their next invoice.

customer_address_city.name: customer_address_city
customer_address_city.comment: City, district, suburb, town, or village.

customer_address_line_1.name: customer_address_line_1
customer_address_line_1.comment: Address line 1 (e.g., street, PO Box, or company name).

customer_address_line_2.name: customer_address_line_2
customer_address_line_2.comment: Address line 2 (e.g., apartment, suite, unit, or building).

customer_balance.name: customer_balance
customer_balance.comment: Current balance, if any, being stored on the customer. If negative, the customer has credit to apply to their next invoice. If positive, the customer has an amount owed that will be added to their next invoice. The balance does not refer to any unpaid invoices; it solely takes into account amounts that have yet to be successfully applied to any invoice. This balance is only taken into account as invoices are finalized.

created_at.name: created_at

currency.name: currency

default_card_id.name: default_card_id
default_card_id.comment: ID for the default card used by the customer.

is_delinquent.name: is_delinquent
is_delinquent.comment: When the customer’s latest invoice is billed by charging automatically, delinquent is true if the invoice’s latest charge is failed. When the customer’s latest invoice is billed by sending an invoice, delinquent is true if the invoice is not paid by its due date.

type_id.name: type_id
type_id.comment: Identifier of the related object type (e.g., coupon ID, promotion code).

amount.name: amount
amount.comment: The amount of discount applied. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

checkout_session_id.name: checkout_session_id
checkout_session_id.comment: The Checkout session that this discount is applied to, if it is applied to a particular session in payment mode.

checkout_session_line_item_id.name: checkout_session_line_item_id
checkout_session_line_item_id.comment: The ID of the specific line item within the checkout session that the discount is applied to.

coupon_id.name: coupon_id
coupon_id.comment: The ID of the coupon applied to create this discount.

credit_note_line_item_id.name: credit_note_line_item_id
credit_note_line_item_id.comment: The ID of the credit note line item associated with this discount.

customer_id.name: customer_id

end_at.name: end_at
end_at.comment: If the coupon has a duration of repeating, the date that this discount will end. If the coupon has a duration of once or forever, this attribute will be null.

invoice_id.name: invoice_id
invoice_id.comment: The invoice that the discount’s coupon was applied to, if it was applied directly to a particular invoice.

invoice_item_id.name: invoice_item_id
invoice_item_id.comment: The invoice item id (or invoice line item id for invoice line items of type=‘subscription’) that the discount’s coupon was applied to, if it was applied directly to a particular invoice item or invoice line item.

promotion_code.name: promotion_code
promotion_code.comment: The promotion code applied to create this discount.

start_at.name: start_at
start_at.comment: The date that the coupon was applied.

subscription_id.name: subscription_id
subscription_id.comment: The subscription that this coupon is applied to, if it is applied to a particular subscription.

metadata.name: stg_stripe__dispute
metadata.comment: Properties belonging to a Stripe dispute.

dispute_amount.name: dispute_amount
dispute_amount.comment: Disputed amount. Usually the amount of the charge, but can differ (usually because of currency fluctuation or because only part of the order is disputed). "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

balance_transaction.name: balance_transaction
balance_transaction.comment: List of zero, one, or two balance transactions that show funds withdrawn and reinstated to your Stripe account as a result of this dispute.

charge_id.name: charge_id
charge_id.comment: ID of the charge that was disputed.

connected_account_id.name: connected_account_id
connected_account_id.comment: Account id associated with this dispute.

dispute_created_at.name: dispute_created_at
dispute_created_at.comment: Time at which the object was created. Measured in seconds since the Unix epoch.

dispute_currency.name: dispute_currency
dispute_currency.comment: Three-letter ISO currency code, in lowercase. Must be a supported currency.

evidence_access_activity_log.name: evidence_access_activity_log
evidence_access_activity_log.comment: Any server or activity logs showing proof that the customer accessed or downloaded the purchased digital product. This information should include IP addresses, corresponding timestamps, and any detailed recorded activity. Has a maximum character count of 20,000.

evidence_billing_address.name: evidence_billing_address
evidence_billing_address.comment: The billing address provided by the customer.

evidence_cancellation_policy.name: evidence_cancellation_policy
evidence_cancellation_policy.comment: (ID of a file upload) Your subscription cancellation policy, as shown to the customer.

evidence_cancellation_policy_disclosure.name: evidence_cancellation_policy_disclosure
evidence_cancellation_policy_disclosure.comment: An explanation of how and when the customer was shown your refund policy prior to purchase. Has a maximum character count of 20,000.

evidence_cancellation_rebuttal.name: evidence_cancellation_rebuttal
evidence_cancellation_rebuttal.comment: A justification for why the customer’s subscription was not canceled. Has a maximum character count of 20,000.

evidence_customer_communication.name: evidence_customer_communication
evidence_customer_communication.comment: (ID of a file upload) Any communication with the customer that you feel is relevant to your case. Examples include emails proving that the customer received the product or service, or demonstrating their use of or satisfaction with the product or service.

evidence_customer_email_address.name: evidence_customer_email_address
evidence_customer_email_address.comment: The email address of the customer.

evidence_customer_purchase_ip.name: evidence_customer_purchase_ip
evidence_customer_purchase_ip.comment: The IP address that the customer used when making the purchase.

evidence_customer_signature.name: evidence_customer_signature
evidence_customer_signature.comment: (ID of a file upload) A relevant document or contract showing the customer’s signature.

evidence_details_due_by.name: evidence_details_due_by
evidence_details_due_by.comment: Date by which evidence must be submitted in order to successfully challenge dispute. Will be 0 if the customer’s bank or credit card company doesn’t allow a response for this particular dispute.

evidence_details_has_evidence.name: evidence_details_has_evidence
evidence_details_has_evidence.comment: Whether evidence has been staged for this dispute.

evidence_details_past_due.name: evidence_details_past_due
evidence_details_past_due.comment: Whether the last evidence submission was submitted past the due date. Defaults to false if no evidence submissions have occurred. If true, then delivery of the latest evidence is not guaranteed.

evidence_details_submission_count.name: evidence_details_submission_count
evidence_details_submission_count.comment: The number of times evidence has been submitted. Typically, you may only submit evidence once.

evidence_duplicate_charge_documentation.name: evidence_duplicate_charge_documentation
evidence_duplicate_charge_documentation.comment: (ID of a file upload) Documentation for the prior charge that can uniquely identify the charge, such as a receipt, shipping label, work order, etc. This document should be paired with a similar document from the disputed payment that proves the two payments are separate.

evidence_duplicate_charge_explanation.name: evidence_duplicate_charge_explanation
evidence_duplicate_charge_explanation.comment: An explanation of the difference between the disputed charge versus the prior charge that appears to be a duplicate. Has a maximum character count of 20,000.

evidence_duplicate_charge_id.name: evidence_duplicate_charge_id
evidence_duplicate_charge_id.comment: The Stripe ID for the prior charge which appears to be a duplicate of the disputed charge.

evidence_product_description.name: evidence_product_description
evidence_product_description.comment: A description of the product or service that was sold. Has a maximum character count of 20,000.

evidence_receipt.name: evidence_receipt
evidence_receipt.comment: (ID of a file upload) Any receipt or message sent to the customer notifying them of the charge.

evidence_refund_policy.name: evidence_refund_policy
evidence_refund_policy.comment: (ID of a file upload) Your refund policy, as shown to the customer.

evidence_refund_policy_disclosure.name: evidence_refund_policy_disclosure
evidence_refund_policy_disclosure.comment: Documentation demonstrating that the customer was shown your refund policy prior to purchase. Has a maximum character count of 20,000.

evidence_refund_refusal_explanation.name: evidence_refund_refusal_explanation
evidence_refund_refusal_explanation.comment: A justification for why the customer is not entitled to a refund. Has a maximum character count of 20,000.

evidence_service_date.name: evidence_service_date
evidence_service_date.comment: The date on which the customer received or began receiving the purchased service, in a clear human-readable format.

evidence_service_documentation.name: evidence_service_documentation
evidence_service_documentation.comment: (ID of a file upload) Documentation showing proof that a service was provided to the customer. This could include a copy of a signed contract, work order, or other form of written agreement.

evidence_shipping_address.name: evidence_shipping_address
evidence_shipping_address.comment: The address to which a physical product was shipped. You should try to include as complete address information as possible.

evidence_shipping_carrier.name: evidence_shipping_carrier
evidence_shipping_carrier.comment: The delivery service that shipped a physical product, such as Fedex, UPS, USPS, etc. If multiple carriers were used for this purchase, please separate them with commas.

evidence_shipping_date.name: evidence_shipping_date
evidence_shipping_date.comment: The date on which a physical product began its route to the shipping address, in a clear human-readable format.

evidence_shipping_documentation.name: evidence_shipping_documentation
evidence_shipping_documentation.comment: (ID of a file upload) Documentation showing proof that a product was shipped to the customer at the same address the customer provided to you. This could include a copy of the shipment receipt, shipping label, etc. It should show the customer’s full shipping address, if possible.

evidence_shipping_tracking_number.name: evidence_shipping_tracking_number
evidence_shipping_tracking_number.comment: The tracking number for a physical product, obtained from the delivery service. If multiple tracking numbers were generated for this purchase, please separate them with commas.

evidence_uncategorized_file.name: evidence_uncategorized_file
evidence_uncategorized_file.comment: (ID of a file upload) Any additional evidence or statements.

evidence_uncategorized_text.name: evidence_uncategorized_text
evidence_uncategorized_text.comment: Any additional evidence or statements. Has a maximum character count of 20,000.

is_charge_refundable.name: is_charge_refundable
is_charge_refundable.comment: Boolean ff true, it is still possible to refund the disputed payment. Once the payment has been fully refunded, no further funds will be withdrawn from your Stripe account as a result of this dispute.

dispute_metadata.name: dispute_metadata
dispute_metadata.comment: Custom metadata added to the record, in JSON string format

dispute_reason.name: dispute_reason
dispute_reason.comment: Reason given by cardholder for dispute. Possible values are bank_cannot_process, check_returned, credit_not_processed, customer_initiated, debit_not_authorized, duplicate, fraudulent, general, incorrect_account_details, insufficient_funds, product_not_received, product_unacceptable, subscription_canceled, or unrecognized.

dispute_status.name: dispute_status
dispute_status.comment: Current status of dispute. Possible values are warning_needs_response, warning_under_review, warning_closed, needs_response, under_review, won, or lost.

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The source where this data was pulled from. If you are making use of the `union_schemas` variable, this will be the source schema. If you are making use of the `union_databases` variable, this will be the source database. If you are not unioning together multiple sources, this will be an empty string.

metadata.name: stg_stripe__invoice
metadata.comment: Invoices are statements of amounts owed by a customer, and are either generated one-off, or generated periodically from a subscription.

default_payment_method_id.name: default_payment_method_id
default_payment_method_id.comment: ID of the default payment method in this invoice.

payment_intent_id.name: payment_intent_id
payment_intent_id.comment: ID of the PaymentIntent associated with this invoice.

subscription_id.name: subscription_id
subscription_id.comment: The ID of the subscription that the invoice pertains to,.

amount_due.name: amount_due
amount_due.comment: Final amount due at this time for this invoice. If the invoice’s total is smaller than the minimum charge amount, for example, or if there is account credit that can be applied to the invoice, the amount_due may be 0. If there is a positive starting_balance for the invoice (the customer owes money), the amount_due will also take that into account. The charge that gets generated for the invoice will be for the amount specified in amount_due. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

amount_paid.name: amount_paid
amount_paid.comment: The amount, that was paid. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

amount_remaining.name: amount_remaining
amount_remaining.comment: The amount remaining, that is due. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

post_payment_credit_notes_amount.name: post_payment_credit_notes_amount
post_payment_credit_notes_amount.comment: Total amount of all post-payment credit notes issued for this invoice. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

pre_payment_credit_notes_amount.name: pre_payment_credit_notes_amount
pre_payment_credit_notes_amount.comment: Total amount of all pre-payment credit notes issued for this invoice. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

subtotal.name: subtotal
subtotal.comment: Total of all subscriptions, invoice items, and prorations on the invoice before any discount or tax is applied.

tax.name: tax
tax.comment: The amount of tax on this invoice. This is the sum of all the tax amounts on this invoice. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

total.name: total
total.comment: Total after discounts and taxes. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

attempt_count.name: attempt_count
attempt_count.comment: Number of payment attempts made for this invoice, from the perspective of the payment retry schedule.

auto_advance.name: auto_advance
auto_advance.comment: Controls whether Stripe will perform automatic collection of the invoice. When false, the invoice’s state will not automatically advance without an explicit action.

billing_reason.name: billing_reason
billing_reason.comment: Indicates the reason why the invoice was created.

charge_id.name: charge_id
charge_id.comment: ID of the latest charge generated for this invoice, if any.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.comment: Time at which the object was created.

currency.name: currency
currency.comment: Three-letter ISO currency code, in lowercase.

customer_id.name: customer_id
customer_id.comment: The ID of the customer who will be billed.

description.name: description
description.comment: An arbitrary string attached to the object. Often useful for displaying to users. Referenced as ‘memo’ in the Dashboard.

due_date.name: due_date
due_date.comment: The date on which payment for this invoice is due. This value will be null for invoices where collection_method=charge_automatically.

metadata.name: metadata
metadata.comment: Custom metadata added to the record, in JSON string format

number.name: number
number.comment: A unique, identifying string that appears on emails sent to the customer for this invoice. This starts with the customer’s unique invoice_prefix if it is specified.

is_paid.name: is_paid
is_paid.comment: Whether payment was successfully collected for this invoice. An invoice can be paid (most commonly) with a charge or with credit from the customer’s account balance.

receipt_number.name: receipt_number
receipt_number.comment: This is the transaction number that appears on email receipts sent for this invoice.

period_start.name: period_start
period_start.comment: Start of the usage period during for which the invoice was created.

period_end.name: period_end
period_end.comment: End of the usage period during for which the invoice was created.

status_transitions_finalized_at.name: status_transitions_finalized_at
status_transitions_finalized_at.comment: The time that the invoice draft was finalized.

status_transitions_marked_uncollectible_.name: status_transitions_marked_uncollectible_at
status_transitions_marked_uncollectible_.comment: The time that the invoice was marked uncollectible.

status_transitions_paid_at.name: status_transitions_paid_at
status_transitions_paid_at.comment: The time that the invoice was paid.

status_transitions_voided_at.name: status_transitions_voided_at
status_transitions_voided_at.comment: The time that the invoice was voided.

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The source where this data was pulled from. If you are making use of the `union_schemas` variable, this will be the source schema. If you are making use of the `union_databases` variable, this will be the source database. If you are not unioning together multiple sources, this will be an empty string.

quantity.name: quantity

metadata.comment: A Payment Intent guides you through the process of collecting a payment from your customer.

amount.name: amount
amount.comment: Amount intended to be collected by this PaymentIntent. A positive integer representing how much to charge in the major currency unit. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

amount_capturable.name: amount_capturable
amount_capturable.comment: Amount that can be captured from this PaymentIntent. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

amount_received.name: amount_received
amount_received.comment: Amount that was collected by this PaymentIntent. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

metadata.name: stg_stripe__price_plan
metadata.comment: This model defines the base price, currency, and billing cycle for recurring purchases of products. Please note that the Prices API replaced the Plans API in Stripe, so if you have migrated and choose to use the Price object then these columns come from the Price object. Otherwise, these will come from the Plan object. For how to configure, refer to the README.

price_plan_id.name: price_plan_id
price_plan_id.comment: The ID of the record. If you have opted to use the Prices API, this will be the ID from the price object. If you opted not to, this will be the ID from the plan object.

is_active.name: is_active
is_active.comment: Whether the plan can be used for new purchases.

unit_amount.name: unit_amount
unit_amount.comment: The unit amount in currency units to be charged, represented as a whole integer if possible. In the Plan source table as Amount.  "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

currency.name: currency
currency.comment: Three-letter ISO currency code, in lowercase.

recurring_interval.name: recurring_interval
recurring_interval.comment: The frequency at which a subscription is billed. One of day, week, month or year. In the Plan table as Interval.

recurring_interval_count.name: recurring_interval_count
recurring_interval_count.comment: The number of intervals between subscription billings. For example, interval_count=3 bills every 3 months. In the plan source table as interval_count.

recurring_usage_type.name: recurring_usage_type
recurring_usage_type.comment: Configures how the quantity per period should be determined. Can be either metered or licensed. licensed automatically bills the quantity set when adding it to a subscription. metered aggregates the total usage based on usage records. Defaults to licensed. In the Plan object as usage_type.

recurring_aggregate_usage.name: recurring_aggregate_usage
recurring_aggregate_usage.comment: Specifies a usage aggregation strategy for prices of usage_type=metered. Allowed values are sum for summing up all usage during a period, last_during_period for using the last usage record reported within a period, last_ever for using the last usage record ever (across period bounds) or max which uses the usage record with the maximum reported usage during a period. Defaults to sum. In the plan source table as aggregate_usage.

metadata.name: metadata
metadata.comment: Custom metadata added to the record, in JSON string format

nickname.name: nickname
nickname.comment: A brief description of the plan, hidden from customers.

product_id.name: product_id
product_id.comment: The id of the product whose pricing this plan determines.

billing_scheme.name: billing_scheme
billing_scheme.comment: Describes how to compute the price per period. Either per_unit or tiered. per_unit indicates that the fixed amount (specified in unit_amount or unit_amount_decimal) will be charged per unit in quantity (for prices with usage_type=licensed), or per unit of total usage (for prices with usage_type=metered). tiered indicates that the unit pricing will be computed using a tiering strategy as defined using the tiers and tiers_mode attributes.

metadata.name: stripe__activity_itemized_2
metadata.comment: Modeled after the [titular report](https://stripe.com/docs/reports/activity#downloading-data) from Stripe, each record represents a balance transaction and additional details such as associated customer, charge, refund, fee, and invoice information. This class of Activity reports allows you to see your payments activity alongside the related fees for the same period of time. Designed specifically for users with Interchange Plus (IC+) pricing, it presents users with fees on the date they incur them. This makes it useful for calculating fee accrual entries and understanding your net payments activity. This itemized report contains a full list of individual transactions.

balance_transaction_id.name: balance_transaction_id
balance_transaction_id.comment: Unique identifier for the related balance transaction.

metadata.name: stripe__balance_change_from_activity_itemized_3
metadata.comment: Modeled after the [titular report](https://stripe.com/docs/reports/balance#schema-balance-change-from-activity-itemized-3) from Stripe, each record represents a balance transaction and additional details such as associated customer, charge, refund, card, and invoice information. This class of Balance reports is similar to a bank statement, helping you to reconcile your Stripe balance at the end of the month. The Balance report is most useful if you treat Stripe like a bank account for accounting purposes, reconciling the balance at the end of each month. If you have automatic payouts enabled and prefer to reconcile the transactions settled in each payout, see the Payout reconciliation report instead. The Balance Change from Activity reports provides a more detailed breakdown of your transactions by reporting category. This section includes all transactions except for payouts that affect your balance, including charges, refunds, disputes, other adjustments, and fees.

Possible values are adjustment, advance, advance_funding, application_fee, application_fee_refund, charge, connect_collection_transfer, issuing_authorization_hold, issuing_authorization_release, issuing_dispute, issuing_transaction, payment, payment_failure_refund, payment_refund, payout, payout_cancel, payout_failure, refund, refund_failure, reserve_transaction, reserved_funds, stripe_fee, stripe_fx_fee, tax_fee, topup, topup_reversal, transfer, transfer_cancel, transfer_failure, or transfer_refund.

balance_transaction_reporting_category.comment: Groupings of transactions types based on how they show in Stripe's reporting

customer_facing_amount.comment: The transaction amount that the customer sees. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. customer_facing_currency.comment: Three-letter ISO currency code that the customer sees.

latest_dispute_amount_won.comment: Latest disputed amount that was won in favor of the merchant. Usually the amount of the charge, but can differ (usually because of currency fluctuation or because only part of the order is disputed). "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

latest_dispute_amount_lost.name: latest_dispute_amount_lost
latest_dispute_amount_lost.comment: Latest disputed amount that was lost and returned to the customer. Usually the amount of the charge, but can differ (usually because of currency fluctuation or because only part of the order is disputed). "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

latest_dispute_amount_under_review.name: latest_dispute_amount_under_review
latest_dispute_amount_under_review.comment: Latest disputed amount that is currently under review by the bank. Usually the amount of the charge, but can differ (usually because of currency fluctuation or because only part of the order is disputed). "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

latest_dispute_amount_needs_response.name: latest_dispute_amount_needs_response
latest_dispute_amount_needs_response.comment: Latest disputed amount that currently needs a response (the dispute has been filed but the merchant has not yet responded with evidence). Usually the amount of the charge, but can differ (usually because of currency fluctuation or because only part of the order is disputed). "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

latest_dispute_amount_warning_closed.name: latest_dispute_amount_warning_closed
latest_dispute_amount_warning_closed.comment: Latest disputed amount that is currently of status `warning_under_closed` (early fraud warning being closed due to no formal dispute). Usually the amount of the charge, but can differ (usually because of currency fluctuation or because only part of the order is disputed). "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

latest_dispute_amount_warning_under_revi.name: latest_dispute_amount_warning_under_review
latest_dispute_amount_warning_under_revi.comment: Latest disputed amount that is currently of status `warning_under_review` (card issuer suspects possible fraud but hasn't yet escalated the situation to a full dispute). Usually the amount of the charge, but can differ (usually because of currency fluctuation or because only part of the order is disputed). "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

latest_dispute_amount_warning_needs_resp.name: latest_dispute_amount_warning_needs_response
latest_dispute_amount_warning_needs_resp.comment: Latest disputed amount that is currently of status `warning_needs_response` (early fraud warning has been escalated into formal dispute or card issuer has requested more information). Usually the amount of the charge, but can differ (usually because of currency fluctuation or because only part of the order is disputed). "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

metadata.name: stripe__ending_balance_reconciliation_itemized_4
metadata.comment: Modeled after the [titular report](https://stripe.com/docs/reports/payout-reconciliation#schema-ending-balance-reconciliation-itemized-4) from Stripe, each record represents an automatic payout and additional details such as associated customer, shipping, card, subscription information. This report belongs to the class of Payout Reconciliation reports. The Payout reconciliation report helps you match the payouts you receive in your bank account with the batches of payments and other transactions that they relate to. The payout reconciliation report is only available for users with automatic payouts enabled, and is optimized for users who prefer to reconcile the transactions included in each payout as a settlement batch. If you use manual payouts or prefer to track and reconcile your Stripe balance like a bank account, see the Balance report instead. To help you decide which report is right for you, see the guide for selecting reports. This Ending Balance Reconciliation section provides a similar breakdown of the transactions that hadn’t been settled as of the report’s end date.

metadata.comment: Modeled after the [titular report](https://stripe.com/docs/reports/balance#schema-payouts-itemized-3) from Stripe, each record represents a payout and its additional details such as expected arrival date and current status. This report belongs to the class of Balance reports. The Balance report is similar to a bank statement, helping you to reconcile your Stripe balance at the end of the month. The Balance report is most useful if you treat Stripe like a bank account for accounting purposes, reconciling the balance at the end of each month. If you have automatic payouts enabled and prefer to reconcile the transactions settled in each payout, see the Payout reconciliation report instead. The Payout Itemized report information on each payout made to your bank account.

In both cases, it's the date the paid-out funds are deducted from your Stripe balance. All dates in the requested timezone, or UTC if not provided.

currency.comment: Three-letter ISO code for the currency in which gross, fee and net are defined.

balance_transaction_id.comment: Unique identifier for the balance transaction. Specifically for Payout, the relation is the ID of the balance transaction that describes the impact of this payout on your account balance.

gross.name: gross
gross.comment: Gross amount of the transaction. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

fee.name: fee
fee.comment: Fees paid for this transaction. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

net.name: net
net.comment: Net amount of the transaction. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

reporting_category.name: reporting_category
reporting_category.comment: Reporting Category is a new categorization of balance transactions, meant to improve on the current type field.

description.name: description
description.comment: An arbitrary string attached to the balance transaction. Often useful for displaying to users.

payout_expected_arrival_date.name: payout_expected_arrival_date
payout_expected_arrival_date.comment: Date the payout is scheduled to arrive in the bank. This factors in delays like weekends or bank holidays.

payout_status.name: payout_status
payout_status.comment: Current status of the payout (paid, pending, in_transit, canceled or failed). A payout will be pending until it is submitted to the bank, at which point it becomes in_transit. It will then change to paid if the transaction goes through. If it does not go through successfully, its status will change to failed or canceled.

payout_reversed_at.name: payout_reversed_at
payout_reversed_at.comment: Typically this field will be empty. However, if the payout's status is canceled or failed, this field will reflect the time at which it entered that status. Times in the requested timezone, or UTC if not provided.

payout_description.name: payout_description
payout_description.comment: An arbitrary string attached to the payout. Often useful for displaying to users.

payout_destination_id.name: payout_destination_id
payout_destination_id.comment: ID of the bank account or card the payout was sent to.

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The source where this data was pulled from. If you are making use of the `union_schemas` variable, this will be the source schema. If you are making use of the `union_databases` variable, this will be the source database. If you are not unioning together multiple sources, this will be an empty string.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stripe__subscription_details
metadata.comment: Each record represents a subscription, enriched with customer details and payment aggregations.

subscription_id.name: subscription_id
subscription_id.comment: Unique identifier for the subscription object.

customer_id.name: customer_id
customer_id.comment: ID of the customer who owns the subscription.

customer_description.name: customer_description
customer_description.comment: Description of the customer who owns the subscription.

customer_email.name: customer_email
customer_email.comment: Email of the customer who owns the subscription.

status.name: status
status.comment: Possible values are incomplete, incomplete_expired, trialing, active, past_due, canceled, or unpaid.

start_date_at.name: start_date_at
start_date_at.comment: Date when the subscription was first created. The date might differ from the created date due to backdating.

ended_at.name: ended_at
ended_at.comment: If the subscription has ended, the date the subscription ended.

billing_cycle_anchor.name: billing_cycle_anchor
billing_cycle_anchor.comment: Determines the date of the first full invoice, and, for plans with month or year intervals, the day of the month for subsequent invoices.

canceled_at.name: canceled_at
canceled_at.comment: If the subscription has been canceled, the date of that cancellation. If the subscription was canceled with cancel_at_period_end, canceled_at will still reflect the date of the initial cancellation request, not the end of the subscription period when the subscription is automatically moved to a canceled state.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.comment: Time at which the subscription object was created.

current_period_start.name: current_period_start
current_period_start.comment: Start of the current period that the subscription has been invoiced for.

current_period_end.name: current_period_end
current_period_end.comment: End of the current period that the subscription has been invoiced for. At the end of this period, a new invoice will be created.

days_until_due.name: days_until_due
days_until_due.comment: Number of days a customer has to pay invoices generated by this subscription. This value will be null for subscriptions where collection_method=charge_automatically.

is_cancel_at_period_end.name: is_cancel_at_period_end
is_cancel_at_period_end.comment: If the subscription has been canceled with the at_period_end flag set to true, cancel_at_period_end on the subscription will be true. You can use this attribute to determine whether a subscription that has a status of active is scheduled to be canceled at the end of the current period.

cancel_at.name: cancel_at
cancel_at.comment: A date in the future at which the subscription will automatically get canceled

number_invoices_generated.name: number_invoices_generated
number_invoices_generated.comment: Number of invoices that have been generated for this subscription.

total_amount_billed.name: total_amount_billed
total_amount_billed.comment: The total amount that has been billed to the customer for this subscription. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

total_amount_paid.name: total_amount_paid
total_amount_paid.comment: The total amount paid by the customer for this subscription. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

total_amount_remaining.name: total_amount_remaining
total_amount_remaining.comment: Any outstanding balance for invoices related to this subscription. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

most_recent_invoice_created_at.name: most_recent_invoice_created_at
most_recent_invoice_created_at.comment: The timestamp of the most recent invoice for this subscription created.

average_invoice_amount.name: average_invoice_amount
average_invoice_amount.comment: The average amount of invoices generated for this subscription. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

average_line_item_amount.name: average_line_item_amount
average_line_item_amount.comment: The average sum of the line items for invoices generated for this subscription. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

avg_num_line_items.name: avg_num_line_items
avg_num_line_items.comment: The average number of line items for invoices generated for this subscription

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The source where this data was pulled from. If you are making use of the `union_schemas` variable, this will be the source schema. If you are making use of the `union_databases` variable, this will be the source database. If you are not unioning together multiple sources, this will be an empty string.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stripe__subscription_item_mrr_report
metadata.comment: Each record represents a subscription item for a given month with monthly recurring revenue (MRR) metrics and movement classification. Tracks MRR changes over time, classifying each month as new, expansion, contraction, churned, reactivation, or unchanged.

source_relation.name: source_relation
source_relation.comment: The source where this data was pulled from. If you are making use of the `union_schemas` variable, this will be the source schema. If you are making use of the `union_databases` variable, this will be the source database. If you are not unioning together multiple sources, this will be an empty string.

subscription_item_id.name: subscription_item_id
subscription_item_id.comment: Unique identifier for the subscription item.

customer_id.name: customer_id
customer_id.comment: ID of the customer who owns the subscription.

product_id.name: product_id
product_id.comment: ID of the product associated with this subscription item.

price_plan_id.name: price_plan_id
price_plan_id.comment: ID of the price plan associated with this subscription item.

subscription_status.name: subscription_status
subscription_status.comment: Current status of the subscription. Possible values are incomplete, incomplete_expired, trialing, active, past_due, canceled, or unpaid.

currency.name: currency
currency.comment: Three-letter ISO currency code, in lowercase.

month_contract_mrr.name: month_contract_mrr
month_contract_mrr.comment: Contracted monthly recurring revenue for this subscription item for this subscription month before discounts are applied. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

month_discount_applied.name: month_discount_applied
month_discount_applied.comment: Discount amount applied to this subscription item for this month, allocated proportionally based on the item's share of the subscription's total contracted MRR. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

month_billed_mrr.name: month_billed_mrr
month_billed_mrr.comment: Net monthly recurring revenue for this subscription item for this subscription month after discounts are applied (month_contract_mrr minus month_discount_applied). "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

prior_month_contract_mrr.name: prior_month_contract_mrr
prior_month_contract_mrr.comment: Contracted monthly recurring revenue for this subscription item for the previous subscription month before discounts are applied. "Values can be expressed either in the smallest currency unit (default) or in the major currency unit (divided by 100), as determined by the `stripe__convert_values` variable. Refer to the [README]((https://github.com/fivetran/dbt_stripe_source?tab=readme-ov-file#enabling-cent-to-dollar-conversion)) for more information."

item_month_number.name: item_month_number
item_month_number.comment: Sequential month number for this subscription item, starting from 1 for the first month.

contract_mrr_type.name: contract_mrr_type
contract_mrr_type.comment: Classification of the contracted MRR change for this month based on month_contract_mrr. Possible values are new (first month with MRR), expansion (MRR increased), contraction (MRR decreased but still positive), churned (MRR went to zero), reactivation (MRR returned after being zero for at least 3 months), unchanged (MRR stayed the same), or unknown.
